alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel modelling approach can estimate spatially explicit species population trends while controlling for interannual confounding in citizen science data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core methodological claim about the Double ML approach and its ability to control interannual confounding; wording and certainty remain consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method produces spatially detailed trend estimates from citizen science data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel modelling approach can estimate spatially explicit species population trends while controlling for interannual confounding in citizen science data.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the result as the modelling approach's ability to produce spatially explicit trends while explicitly noting control for confounding, which clarifies the preprint's statement about spatially detailed estimates.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trend estimates distinguished between spatially constant and spatially varying trends at a 27km resolution with low error rates on the estimated direction of population change.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There were low error rates on the estimated direction of population change and high correlations on the estimated magnitude of population change.
alignments.rationale: The publication separates and quantifies error rates and magnitude correlations; the preprint combined distinguishing trend types and low direction error at a given resolution. The publication clarifies the error and magnitude evaluation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Estimating spatially explicit trends while accounting for confounding in citizen science data can help fill important information gaps.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ability to estimate spatially explicit trends while accounting for confounding has the potential to fill important information gaps in population trend estimation.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert that estimating spatially explicit trends while controlling confounding can fill information gaps; the publication restates this conclusion directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Grobid-superconductors is a solution for automatically extracting superconductor material names and respective properties from text.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Grobid-superconductors is a solution for automatically extracting superconductor material names and respective properties from text.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using Grobid-superconductors, a database of 40324 materials and properties records from 37700 papers was built.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using Grobid-superconductors, a database of 40,324 materials and properties records was built from 37,700 papers.
alignments.rationale: The numeric database build claim is carried forward unchanged (formatting differences only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extracted material information includes name, chemical formula, and material class, along with shape, doping, substitution variables for components, and substrate.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extracted material information includes name, chemical formula, and material class, along with characteristics such as shape, doping, and substitution variables.
alignments.rationale: The publication lists the same extracted material attributes; substrate is omitted from the shorter phrasing but the core attributes match.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The properties extracted include the Tc superconducting critical temperature and, when available, applied pressure with the Tc measurement method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The properties extracted include the Tc superconducting critical temperature and, when available, applied pressure with the Tc measurement method.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the properties-extraction claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method can effectively improve the accuracy of depth estimation in occlusion and smooth regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method can effectively improve the accuracy of disparity estimation in occlusion and smooth regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording differs only by 'depth' vs 'disparity' terminology; the claim about improved accuracy in occlusion/smooth regions is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two-step adaptive matching algorithm reduces mismatches and redundant calculations by selecting effective matching windows. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The two-step adaptive matching algorithm reduces mismatches and redundant calculations by selecting effective matching windows. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and certainty in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High-precision depth estimation from 4D light field data is achieved. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: High-precision disparity estimation from 4D light field data is achieved. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same result claim with only terminology change from 'depth' to 'disparity'.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The matching entropy criterion is used to adaptively select the size and shape of matching windows, as well as the visible viewpoints. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The matching entropy criterion is used to adaptively select the size and shape of matching windows, as well as the visible viewpoints. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Claim is verbatim between versions with same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Vision-Language Transformer (VLT) framework enhances the holistic understanding of vision-language features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Vision-Language Transformer (VLT) framework enhances the holistic understanding of vision-language features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim and certainty in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Query Generation Module dynamically produces multiple sets of input-specific queries to represent diverse comprehensions of language expression. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Query Generation Module dynamically produces multiple sets of input-specific queries to represent diverse comprehensions of language expression. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same description of the module and role in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Query Balance Module selectively fuses the corresponding responses of the set of queries to generate better masks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Query Balance Module selectively fuses the corresponding responses of the set of queries to generate better masks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Module functionality is stated identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach achieves new state-of-the-art referring segmentation results consistently on five datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach achieves new state-of-the-art referring segmentation results consistently on five datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result claim identical in scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Vox-Fusion achieves better accuracy and completeness than previous methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Vox-Fusion achieves better accuracy and completeness than previous methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result is stated verbatim and with same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Vox-Fusion can be applied to practical scenarios without prior knowledge of the environment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system supports dynamic expansion and can track and map arbitrary scenes without prior knowledge of the environment. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication provides more concrete description of what 'applied without prior knowledge' entails (tracking/mapping arbitrary scenes and dynamic expansion), so the claim is clarified with additional specifics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed multi-process framework supports real-time performance for various applications. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A high-performance multi-process framework is proposed to speed up the method for real-time applications. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is the same (multi-process framework for real-time use) but the publication uses a lower certainty and frames it as proposed to speed up for real-time, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Vox-Fusion is suitable for use in augmented reality and virtual reality applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Vox-Fusion can be used in augmented reality and virtual reality applications. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same application claim but the publication expresses it with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed risk-aware data-driven bid optimization model maximizes the expected profit for the advertiser. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed risk-aware data-driven bid optimization model maximizes the expected profit for the advertiser. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical high-certainty conclusion in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model effectively controls the risk of overspending the budget. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model effectively controls the risk of overspending the budget. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The risk-averse method achieves a competitive level of profit compared to the risk-neutral model. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The risk-averse method achieves a competitive level of profit compared to the risk-neutral model. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same comparative result with same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A parametrized closed-form expression for the optimal bidding strategy is derived using Lagrangian relaxation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A parametrized closed-form expression for the optimal bidding strategy is derived using Lagrangian relaxation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution unchanged and stated identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Gated Multi-scale Temporal Convolutional Network (GM-TCNet) effectively constructs a novel emotional causality representation learning component. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Gated Multi-scale Temporal Convolutional Network (GM-TCNet) demonstrates a novel emotional causality representation learning component that effectively captures the dynamics of emotion across the time domain. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GM-TCNet captures the dynamics of emotion across the time domain using a dilated causal convolution layer and gating mechanism. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Gated Multi-scale Temporal Convolutional Network (GM-TCNet) demonstrates a novel emotional causality representation learning component that effectively captures the dynamics of emotion across the time domain. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that GM-TCNet maintains the highest performance in most cases compared to state-of-the-art techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GM-TCNet achieves an average relative improvement of +0.90% to +18.50% on weighted average recall and +0.55% to +20.15% on unweighted average recall compared to state-of-the-art techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication provides quantified performance improvements, refining the preprint's qualitative 'highest performance' claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model utilizes a single type of feature, Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC), as inputs to generate high-level features for the Speech Emotion Recognition task. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Federated learning (FL) enables remote clients to collaboratively train a model without exposing their private data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This general definition/claim about FL appears in the preprint but is not present among the publication claims, so it was removed from the published claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model performance usually degrades in FL due to the heterogeneous data generated by clients of diverse characteristics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about performance degradation due to heterogeneity is stated in the preprint but does not appear in the publication claims, indicating removal.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FedIntR provides a more fine-grained regularization by integrating the representations of intermediate layers into the local training process. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FedIntR provides a more fine-grained regularization by integrating the representations of intermediate layers into the local training process. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: FedIntR can achieve equivalent or higher performance compared to the state-of-the-art approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: FedIntR computes a regularization term that encourages the closeness between the intermediate layer representations of the local and global models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Many existing methods attribute unfairness in healthcare datasets solely to the data collection process, which lacks substantial evidence. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The pilot study demonstrates that the presence of unobserved confounders exacerbates disparities in healthcare. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Parity Medical Deconfounder (PriMeD) framework effectively addresses disparity issues in healthcare datasets by using a Conditional Variational Autoencoder to learn latent factors. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Articulatory Generator more closely mimics human speech production by learning to generate articulatory representations in a fully unsupervised manner. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Articulatory analysis suggests that the network learns to control articulators in a similar manner to humans during speech production. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Acoustic analysis of the outputs suggests that the network learns to generate words that are both present and absent in the training distribution. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The FAS-UNet integrates image data and mathematical models through learning a few convolution kernels. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach retrieves 3.28 out of 3.4 hours of previously unusable recordings. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: An adversary can extract/reconstruct the exact training samples from pre-trained Large Language Models, which can lead to revealing personally identifiable information. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text:
The proposed 
ewtune framework for fine-tuning LLMs based on Edgeworth accountant offers finite-sample privacy guarantees. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed multi-scale and multi-granularity feature learning network (MMFL-net) significantly improves instance-level image retrieval in the fashion industry. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the layer's role in interpretability but omits the specific survey-based assessment described in the preprint, so the claim is clarified/focused in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method for assessing spatial and temporal robustness involves simulating noise in the perception system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A method for assessing spatial and temporal robustness is introduced by simulating noise in the perception system.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication describe the same robustness assessment method of simulating perception noise with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) diagnosis is often hindered by reliance on spirometric examination and functional airway limitation, leading to underdiagnosis in early stages.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) diagnosis is often compromised due to reliance on spirometric examination and functional airway limitation, leading to underdiagnosis, especially in early stages.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same diagnostic limitation and underdiagnosis concern with equivalent meaning and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models trained on CT images from one data center do not generalize well to images from another center due to heterogeneous syndromes and distinct phenotypes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning models trained on CT images from one data center do not generalize well to images from another center due to heterogeneous syndromes and distinct phenotypes.
alignments.rationale: The claim about poor generalization across centers is carried over verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed personalized federated learning (PFL) method based on vision transformer (ViT) effectively addresses the performance degradation in non-IID COPD CTs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed personalized federated learning (PFL) method based on vision transformer (ViT) effectively addresses the performance degradation in COPD CTs that are not independent and identically distributed (Non-IID).
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that the ViT-based PFL method mitigates Non-IID performance degradation; wording is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first proposal of a PFL framework specifically for ViT to identify COPD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first proposal of a personalized federated learning framework specifically for vision transformers to identify COPD.
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim (first PFL for ViT in COPD) is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evaluation of a dataset curated from six medical centers demonstrates that the proposed method outperforms existing PFL approaches for convolutional neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation of a dataset curated from six medical centers demonstrates that the proposed method outperforms existing PFL approaches for convolutional neural networks.
alignments.rationale: Evaluation results on the six-center dataset and the stated outperforming of existing CNN-based PFL approaches are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adversarial face examples pose a significant threat to the security of society.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adversarial face examples pose a significant threat to the security of society.
alignments.rationale: The high-level threat statement is identically preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The concept of hard models can improve the transferability of adversarial face examples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The concept of hard models can improve the transferability of adversarial face examples.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Beneficial Perturbation Feature Augmentation Attack (BPFA) reduces the overfitting of adversarial examples to surrogate face recognition models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BPFA reduces the overfitting of adversarial examples to surrogate face recognition models.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly repeats the mechanism-level claim that BPFA reduces overfitting to surrogate models.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The beneficial perturbation feature augmentation attack (BPFA) significantly boosts the transferability of adversarial attacks on face recognition models.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Event-based image representations are fundamentally different from traditional dense images. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm optimizes pointwise map smoothness alongside desirable functional map properties such as bijectivity. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm is robust against state preparation and measurement error and does not require eigenstates or thermal states. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed beam management scheme based on federated learning can theoretically protect user privacy while reducing handoff cost.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed beam management scheme based on federated learning can theoretically protect user privacy while reducing handoff cost.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the theoretical privacy protection and handoff cost reduction claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adopting a data cleaning technique in the local model training for the beam management scheme aims to strengthen user privacy protection and improve learning convergence speed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adopting a data cleaning technique in the local model training for the beam management scheme improves the learning convergence speed.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the convergence-speed benefit but drops the claim about strengthening privacy, narrowing the scope of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulation results demonstrate the performance gain of the proposed beam management scheme.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulation results demonstrate the performance gain of the proposed beam management scheme.
alignments.rationale: Simulation-based performance claims are present unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ImmFusion is the first mmWave-RGB fusion solution to reconstruct 3D human bodies in all weather conditions robustly.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ImmFusion is the first mmWave-RGB fusion solution to reconstruct 3D human bodies in all weather conditions robustly.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ImmFusion can efficiently utilize the information of two modalities to achieve a robust 3D human body reconstruction in all weather conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ImmFusion can efficiently utilize the information of two modalities to achieve a robust 3D human body reconstruction in all weather conditions.
alignments.rationale: The multimodal robustness claim remains identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of ImmFusion is significantly superior to that of state-of-the-art Transformer-based LiDAR-camera fusion methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of ImmFusion is significantly superior to that of state-of-the-art Transformer-based LiDAR-camera fusion methods.
alignments.rationale: The comparative accuracy result is preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The choice of the normalisation strategy plays a key role on the classifier performances in Domain Adaptation (DA) scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The choice of the normalization strategy plays a key role in classifier performances in Domain Adaptation scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core result about normalization strategy importance with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In several cases, the use of only an appropriate normalisation schema outperforms the DA technique.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using an appropriate normalization schema often outperforms the Domain Adaptation technique.
alignments.rationale: The publication slightly reframes the frequency wording ("in several cases" → "often") but preserves the conclusion that normalization alone can outperform DA.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EEG signals are highly non-stationary both over time and between different subjects.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims focus on normalization performance metrics and dataset results; the general statement about EEG non-stationarity does not appear among the publication claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: In a cross-subject context, a proper normalization strategy alone can achieve an accuracy of 81.52 ± 7.26% for the SEED dataset.
alignments.rationale: This specific dataset performance result appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint claims list.

alignments.pub_claim_text: In a cross-session context, the accuracy of 86.56 ± 8.15% can be reached for the SEED dataset using normalization.
alignments.rationale: A dataset-specific cross-session accuracy result is included in the publication but not in the preprint's summarized claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The best cross-subject performance for the DEAP dataset achieved using only normalization was 39.33 ± 14.08%.
alignments.rationale: This DEAP dataset performance figure is reported in the publication but was not listed among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the HRP portfolio is superior to that of its Eigen counterpart on both training and test data for the majority of the sectors studied. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the HRP portfolio is superior to that of its Eigen counterpart on both training and test data for the majority of the sectors studied. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance result verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A systematic approach to portfolio optimization using the hierarchical risk parity algorithm and the Eigen portfolio was developed. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A systematic approach to portfolio optimization using the hierarchical risk parity algorithm and the Eigen portfolio was developed. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement appears unchanged in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The portfolios were built following two approaches to historical stock prices from Jan 1, 2016, to Dec 31, 2020. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The portfolios were built using historical stock prices from January 1, 2016, to December 31, 2020. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the data/time window and portfolio-building claim with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The current state-of-the-art mechanism, Course Match, has a major shortcoming due to students making significant mistakes when reporting their preferences. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The current state-of-the-art mechanism, Course Match, has a major shortcoming due to students making significant mistakes when reporting their preferences. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new mechanism, Machine Learning-powered Course Match (MLCM), significantly increases both average and minimum student utility by 7%-11% and 17%-29%, respectively, with only ten comparison queries. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new mechanism, Machine Learning-powered Course Match (MLCM), significantly increases both average and minimum student utility by 7%--11% and 17%--29%, respectively, with only ten comparison queries. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric improvement and experimental condition are preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MLCM is robust to changes in the environment and minimizes the risk of upgrading to it while making the upgrade process simple for universities and seamless for their students. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MLCM is robust to changes in the environment and minimizes the risk of upgrading to it while making the upgrade process simple for universities and seamless for their students. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward this higher-level conclusion without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new parallel Bayesian optimization paradigms significantly reduce the required search time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new parallel Bayesian optimization paradigms significantly reduce the required search time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about reduced search time is carried over verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approaches increase the probability of finding a global solution compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approaches increase the probability of finding a global solution compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim about improved global-solution probability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing modifications to the Bayesian optimization framework are limited in the degree of parallelization they can achieve. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing modifications to the Bayesian optimization framework are limited in the degree of parallelization they can achieve. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation on prior parallelization capacity is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods partition the design space by following the level sets of the performance function. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach partitions the design space by following the level sets of the performance function. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution about partitioning by level sets is preserved with only minor wording differences (methods vs approach).

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach exploits the partially separable structure of the performance function. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This contribution (exploiting partial separability) is present in the publication but has no counterpart in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed neural-network-based method can derive the mineral modal and chemical compositions of silicate materials from their visible and near-infrared spectra. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network trained with real silicate samples and their mixtures is applicable for a quantitative mineral evaluation of spectra of asteroids that are rich in dry silicates. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the method as trained on real samples and applicable specifically to asteroids rich in dry silicates, narrowing and clarifying the original broader claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The individual classification results of the model are mostly within 10 percentage-point intervals around the correct values. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modal abundances and mineral chemistry of common silicates (olivine and pyroxene) can be derived with an accuracy better than 10 percentage points. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same accuracy result in a more explicit, application-focused form (modal abundances and chemistry of common silicates).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predicted mineral chemical composition of S-type and Q-type asteroids agrees with the chemical composition of ordinary chondrites. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predicted mineral chemical composition of S-type and Q-type asteroids agree with the chemical composition of ordinary chondrites. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content of the prediction is the same, but the publication marks the claim with reduced certainty (high -> medium).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modal abundances of V-type and A-type asteroids show a dominant contribution of orthopyroxene and olivine, respectively. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modal abundances of V-type and A-type asteroids show a dominant contribution of orthopyroxene and olivine, respectively. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific modal-abundance result is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictions of the mineral modal composition of S-type and Q-type asteroids show an apparent depletion of olivine related to the attenuation of its diagnostic absorptions with space weathering. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our predictions of the mineral modal composition of S-type and Q-type asteroids show an apparent depletion of olivine related to the attenuation of its diagnostic absorptions with space weathering. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this observation with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed structural relationship graph representation learning framework effectively measures the similarity of two structural relationships in multimodal images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed structural relationship graph representation learning framework effectively measures the similarity of two structural relationships in multimodal images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The central framework claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structural relationship graph convolutional autoencoder (SR-GCAE) learns robust and representative features from graphs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structural relationship graph convolutional autoencoder (SR-GCAE) learns robust and representative features from graphs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This methodological result is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results on five datasets with different modal combinations demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results on six datasets with different modal combinations demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experiments in the publication include one additional dataset, so the empirical claim is revised and broadened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An adaptive fusion strategy is presented to fuse the two difference images generated from the similarity levels. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this adaptive fusion strategy claim; it is absent in the published claims list.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Two loss functions are introduced to ensure that the learned representations are applicable for structural relationship similarity measurement. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The introduction of two loss functions appears as a new publication contribution not present in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CaiRL Environment Toolkit is a more efficient and sustainable alternative for training learning agents compared to existing toolkits.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CaiRL Environment Toolkit is a more efficient and sustainable alternative for training learning agents compared to existing toolkits.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level contribution claim verbatim, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CaiRL is built using C++, which allows it to operate orders of magnitude faster than the Python-based OpenAI Gym.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CaiRL is built in C++ and operates orders of magnitude faster than OpenAI Gym, which is built in Python.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same implementation and performance comparison between C++ CaiRL and Python OpenAI Gym with equivalent strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CaiRL can significantly reduce climate emissions associated with running reinforcement learning experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CaiRL can significantly reduce climate emissions associated with running reinforcement learning experiments.
alignments.rationale: The emission-reduction conclusion appears identically in the publication with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CaiRL is the first reinforcement learning toolkit to include built-in JVM and Flash support for running legacy flash games.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about built-in JVM and Flash support does not appear in the published claims and thus was removed from the final claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CaiRL can serve as a drop-in replacement for OpenAI Gym, providing significantly faster training speeds due to reduced environment computation time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CaiRL serves as a drop-in replacement for OpenAI Gym, enabling significantly faster training speeds due to reduced environment computation time.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the drop-in replacement claim with equivalent wording and asserted performance benefits.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lightweight electromyography sensors can inform and establish a robust understanding of users' complex hand activities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lightweight electromyography sensors can inform and establish a robust understanding of users' complex hand activities.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our interface can decode finger-wise forces in real-time with 3.3% mean error and generalize to new users with little calibration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our interface can decode finger-wise forces in real-time with 3.3% mean error and generalize to new users with little calibration.
alignments.rationale: Identical quantitative result and generalization claim appear in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Human perception of virtual objects' physical properties, such as stiffness, can be significantly enhanced by our interface.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Human perception of virtual objects' physical properties, such as stiffness, can be significantly enhanced by our interface.
alignments.rationale: The perceptual enhancement claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our interface enables ubiquitous control via finger tapping.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our interface enables ubiquitous control via finger tapping.
alignments.rationale: The control-via-finger-tapping claim is retained without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings of this research aim to push forward research towards more realistic physicality in future VR/AR.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings of this research aim to push forward research towards more realistic physicality in future VR/AR.
alignments.rationale: The concluding research-advancement statement appears unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presented approach achieves a convincing performance in the classification of unknown repetitions of already known subjects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presented deep learning approach is capable of performing complex motion analytic tasks based on inertial measurement unit data.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's specific claim of 'convincing performance' on a defined task is generalized in the publication to a broader capability statement, reducing the strength and specificity of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained network is yet unable to achieve consistent performance on the data of previously unknown subjects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained network is unable to achieve consistent performance on the data of previously unknown subjects.
alignments.rationale: The substance of the limitation is the same, but the publication states it with higher certainty (medium -> high), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the network differs significantly depending on the exercise it is trained for.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the network differs depending on the exercise it is trained for.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries the same observation about performance varying by exercise with comparable certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The observed performance degradation on the data of unknown subjects is comparable to publications of other research groups that relied on classical machine learning methods.
alignments.rationale: This comparative contextualization does not appear in the preprint and is introduced in the publication as an added conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Transfer learning methods could be used to compensate for performance differences between exercises.
alignments.rationale: The suggestion to apply transfer learning is a new recommendation in the published version and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper introduces a novel evaluation methodology for entity resolution algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article introduces a novel evaluation methodology for entity resolution algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The core contribution claim is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methodology accounts for sampling biases in performance estimators.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methodology accounts for sampling biases in performance estimators.
alignments.rationale: The methodological claim about accounting for sampling bias is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach provides a representative picture of PatentsView's disambiguation performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach provides a representative picture of PatentsView’s disambiguation performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim about providing a representative performance picture with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology informs PatentsView's users of the reliability of the data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results inform PatentsView’s users of the reliability of the data.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about informing users of data reliability appears in the published results with the same implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach allows for the comparison of competing disambiguation algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about enabling comparison of competing algorithms is not present among the publication's listed claims and appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Labeled audio data is insufficient to build satisfying speech recognition systems for most languages.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Labeled audio data is insufficient to build satisfying speech recognition systems for most languages.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion about labeled data scarcity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The representation ability of self-supervised pre-trained models is extremely beneficial in zero-resource phoneme recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The representation ability of self-supervised pre-trained models is extremely beneficial in zero-resource phoneme recognition.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claim about benefits of self-supervised pretrained models unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper is the first attempt to extend the use of pre-trained models into word-level zero-resource speech recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper is the first attempt to extend the use of pretrained models into word-level zero-resource speech recognition.
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments show that this method can achieve less than 20% word error rate on some languages.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments show that this method can achieve less than 20% word error rate on some languages.
alignments.rationale: The experimental result is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average error rate on 8 languages is 33.77%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average error rate on 8 languages is 33.77%.
alignments.rationale: The reported average error rate is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Textual datasets present new challenges to the Explainable AI (ExAI) community due to the lack of input structure and the opacity of models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep networks are often used as black-box models, which limits their adoption in critical decision-making fields like medicine and law due to a lack of interpretability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized challenges specific to textual datasets and model opacity; the publication frames the issue more generally as black-box deep networks limiting adoption in critical domains, a broader but related framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent methods have been developed to provide satisfactory explanations for Natural Language Processing (NLP) models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent methods have been developed to provide satisfactory explanations for Natural Language Processing (NLP) models, addressing challenges specific to textual data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint claim but explicitly notes these methods address textual-data-specific challenges, adding clarification without changing the claim's core.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey categorizes explainability methods into three levels: input-level, processing-level, and output-level. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The survey presented in this work categorizes explainability methods for NLP models into three levels: input level (word embeddings), processing level (inner workings), and output level (models' decisions). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert the same three-level categorization; the publication adds brief examples but does not alter the categorization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper details different evaluation approaches for interpretability methods in the NLP field. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a need for a comprehensive framework to rigorously evaluate explainability methods in the NLP field, as current methods have not been studied in such a context. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The preprint claimed the paper details evaluation approaches, whereas the publication emphasizes a gap and need for a comprehensive evaluation framework, shifting the claim from contribution to identified limitation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The authors propose promising future research directions for ExAI in the NLP field. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's main claims do not present or restate explicit proposed future research directions, so this preprint claim is not reflected in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When treewidth or hypertree width is bounded by a constant, the Constraint Satisfaction Problem (CSP) becomes solvable in polynomial time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When treewidth or hypertree width is bounded by a constant, the Constraint Satisfaction Problem (CSP) becomes solvable in polynomial time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same formal result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The order of the polynomial in the running time of CSP depends on the width, which is unavoidable. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The problem is not fixed-parameter tractable when parameterized by treewidth or hypertree width. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's informal 'unavoidable dependence on width' is made formal in the publication as non-fixed-parameter tractability; this refines the conclusion to a stronger, more formal statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of thresholds enhances tree and hypertree width, allowing decompositions to consider computational costs associated with CSP instances. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of thresholds enhances tree and hypertree width by incorporating computational cost information for CSP instances. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution with essentially the same content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Threshold treewidth and hypertree width lead to fixed-parameter algorithms for CSP and other more general problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Threshold treewidth and hypertree width lead to fixed-parameter algorithms for CSP and other more general problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core technical result is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Theoretical results are complemented with experimental evaluations of heuristics and exact methods based on SAT/SMT encodings. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Theoretical results are complemented with experimental evaluations of heuristics and exact methods based on SAT/SMT encodings. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the experimental component as stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing zero-shot learning models produce imbalanced semantic predictions, performing well for some semantics but poorly for others. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing zero-shot learning models produce imbalanced semantic predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication state the same core empirical observation about imbalanced semantic predictions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Imbalanced zero-shot learning has unique challenges, including the correlation of imbalanced predictions with semantic label values rather than sample numbers. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Imbalanced zero-shot learning has unique challenges related to the correlation of predictions with semantic label values and differing error distributions between classes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the diagnostic to include differing error distributions between classes, clarifying and slightly broadening the characterization of challenges.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Re-balanced Mean-Squared Error (ReMSE) effectively alleviates the imbalance in semantic prediction and outperforms many state-of-the-art zero-shot learning methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Re-balanced Mean-Squared Error (ReMSE) loss effectively addresses the imbalance in semantic predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the corrective effect of ReMSE on imbalance; the outperforming claim is present elsewhere in the paper but here the matched publication claim focuses on addressing imbalance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The formalization of zero-shot learning as an imbalanced regression problem provides empirical evidence for the relationship between semantic labels and imbalanced predictions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ReMSE is theoretically well established based on a series of analyses conducted. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized an empirical formalization; the publication presents stronger theoretical analyses establishing ReMSE, shifting from empirical framing to theoretical reinforcement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Anchors explanations are more aligned with human judgment and can be more confident in extracting supporting rationales. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Anchors explanations are more aligned with human judgment and can be more confident in extracting supporting rationales. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical result about Anchors explanations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reasoning complexity of sentiment is shown to thwart ExAI methods from extracting supporting evidence. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reasoning complexity of sentiment is shown to thwart ExAI methods from extracting supporting evidence. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved in the publication with similar framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a remarkable discrepancy between the results of different explainability methods on various architectures, suggesting the need for consolidation to observe enhanced performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A remarkable discrepancy is discerned between the results of different explainability methods on the various architectures, suggesting the need for consolidation to observe enhanced performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the observation about discrepancies between methods and the suggested consolidation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transformers exhibit better explainability than convolutional and recurrent architectures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transformers are shown to exhibit better explainability than convolutional and recurrent architectures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the comparative result regarding transformer explainability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work paves the way towards designing more interpretable NLP models and enabling a common evaluation ground for their relative strengths and robustness. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work paves the way towards designing more interpretable NLP models and enabling a common evaluation ground for their relative strengths and robustness. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the contribution claim about enabling more interpretable models and common evaluation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is impossible to achieve sublinear α-regret for any α<2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is impossible to achieve sublinear α < 2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The impossibility result is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With full feedback, sublinear 2-regret is achievable. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: With full feedback, sublinear 2-regret is achievable. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the achievability claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With a single price and partial feedback, one cannot get sublinear α-regret for any constant α. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: With a single price and partial feedback, one cannot get sublinear α-regret for any constant α. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same negative result for single-price partial feedback.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Posting two prices even with one-bit feedback achieves sublinear 2-regret. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Posting two prices even with one-bit feedback achieves sublinear 2-regret. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the positive result for two-price one-bit feedback setting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a provable separation in the 2-regret bounds between full and partial feedback. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication replaces an asserted broad "survey" with a more limited description of particular features, which narrows and revises the original claim and shows lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new result of convergence for parallel or distributed asynchronous iterative algorithms with flexible communication for convex optimization problems and machine learning is proposed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new result of convergence for parallel or distributed asynchronous iterative algorithms with flexible communication for convex optimization problems and machine learning is proposed.
alignments.rationale: The convergence result appears unchanged and is carried into the publication with similar emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ParaDime provides a common interface for specifying inter-item relationships and transformations in parametric dimensionality reduction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ParaDime provides a common interface for specifying inter-item relationships and transformations in parametric dimensionality reduction.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ParaDime unifies parametric versions of dimensionality reduction techniques such as metric MDS, t-SNE, and UMAP.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ParaDime unifies parametric versions of dimensionality reduction techniques such as metric MDS, t-SNE, and UMAP.
alignments.rationale: The unification claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ease of customization in ParaDime makes it suitable for experimenting with hybrid classification/embedding models and supervised dimensionality reduction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ease of customization in ParaDime makes it suitable for experimenting with hybrid classification/embedding models and supervised dimensionality reduction.
alignments.rationale: The result about customization and suitability is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ParaDime opens up new possibilities for visualizing high-dimensional data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ParaDime opens up new possibilities for visualizing high-dimensional data.
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim is preserved without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: JuryGCN is the first frequentist-based approach for quantifying the uncertainty of Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN).
alignments.pub_claim_text: JuryGCN is the first frequentist-based approach for quantifying the uncertainty of Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN).
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: JuryGCN quantifies the uncertainty of a node as the width of a confidence interval using a jackknife estimator.
alignments.pub_claim_text: JuryGCN quantifies the uncertainty of a node as the width of a confidence interval using a jackknife estimator.
alignments.rationale: The methodological claim about using a jackknife estimator is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed JuryGCN can quantify uncertainty deterministically without modifying the GCN architecture or introducing additional parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed JuryGCN can quantify uncertainty deterministically without modifying the GCN architecture or introducing additional parameters.
alignments.rationale: The claim about deterministic quantification without architecture changes or extra parameters is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experimental evaluation on real-world datasets demonstrates the efficacy of JuryGCN in active learning and semi-supervised node classification tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experimental evaluation on real-world datasets demonstrates the efficacy of JuryGCN in active learning and semi-supervised node classification tasks.
alignments.rationale: The experimental-evaluation claim remains the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method based on the SARAH variance reduction technique achieves linear convergence to a solution for strongly monotone problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method based on the SARAH variance reduction technique achieves linear convergence to a solution for strongly monotone problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the identical technical claim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stochastic finite-sum cocoercive variational inequalities are a significant class of problems in the context of machine learning applications. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stochastic finite-sum cocoercive variational inequalities are a significant class of problems in the context of machine learning applications. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is carried forward verbatim with no change in scope or tone.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments confirm the importance and practical applicability of the proposed approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental conclusion is the same in the publication, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ControlVAE is a novel model-based framework for learning generative motion control policies based on variational autoencoders. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ControlVAE is a novel model-based framework for learning generative motion control policies based on variational autoencoders. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ControlVAE enables the generation of realistic human behaviors by sampling in the latent space. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: Same result claim with only minor wording changes; scope and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learnable world model in ControlVAE effectively captures the unknown dynamics of the simulation system. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Projective Transformation Rectification Network (PTRN) is the first work to predict the projective transformation matrix as the learning goal for photo rectification. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Projective Transformation Rectification Network (PTRN) automatically rectifies the projective transformation of chest X-ray photos by predicting the projective transformation matrix. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach won first place in the CheXphoto smartphone-captured CXR photos classification competition with a significant performance improvement (0.850 AUC compared to 0.762 AUC for the second-best). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: This competition result is reported identically, including the AUC numbers and ranking.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of PTRN successfully achieves classification performance on spatially transformed CXR photos equivalent to that on high-quality digital CXR images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach successfully achieves the same performance level of digital CXR classification, with an AUC of 0.893 on CXR photo classification. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PTRN can eliminate all negative impacts of projective transformation on CXR photos. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's absolute claim that PTRN eliminates all negative impacts is softened in the publication to reporting equivalent performance levels and specific AUCs; this narrows and tempers the original, more categorical conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Energy efficiency should be included as an additional performance criterion in neural architecture search (NAS). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The EC-NAS benchmark provides data on energy consumption for varied architectures and is available in an open-source format. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings indicate that multi-objective optimization algorithms can balance energy usage and accuracy in deep learning models. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research suggests the feasibility of identifying energy-lean architectures with little or no compromise in performance. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EC-NAS demonstrates the feasibility of identifying energy-lean architectures with little or no compromise in performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed recurrent convolutional network based framework for face super-resolution progressively introduces both global shape and local texture information. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Landmarks information and facial action units (AUs) information are extracted from the outputs of the first and second steps of the network, rather than from low-resolution input. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The article examines various tasks including unconditional synthesis, classification, conditional synthesis, segmentation, denoising, detection, and registration.

alignments.pub_claim_text: KAIROS maximizes query throughput while meeting QoS targets and cost budget constraints.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results show the prospect of accurate deterioration forecast using a minimum feature set that can be relatively easily obtained using wearable devices and self-reported patient information.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed LACV-Net effectively captures both local and global information for large-scale point cloud semantic segmentation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LACV-Net includes a local adaptive feature augmentation module that learns the similarity of centroids and neighboring points to enhance local context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The comprehensive VLAD module in LACV-Net fuses local features across multiple layers, scales, and resolutions to create a global description vector.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Comprehensive Vector of Locally Aggregated Descriptors (C-VLAD) module captures global context more efficiently by fusing local features across multiple resolution representations.
alignments.rationale: The publication names and describes the same VLAD/C-VLAD module with equivalent functionality of fusing multi-resolution local features to capture global context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The aggregation loss function in LACV-Net optimizes segmentation boundaries by constraining adaptive weights from the LAFA module.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Aggregation Loss Function guides convergence towards centroid features, further mitigating local perception ambiguity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LACV-Net outperforms state-of-the-art networks on large-scale benchmark datasets such as S3DIS, Toronto3D, and SensatUrban.
